preview_text:
The model favors Mexico heavily, assigning a 71% win probability against a Czech Republic chance of 7%. The gap between the two reflects a substantial model confidence in the Mexican side, though the underdog retains a meaningful probability of an upset or stalemate.

The draw enters at 23%, suggesting the model anticipates a competitive but tilted contest. Mexico's advantage does not translate into a rout scenario; nearly a quarter of outcomes are expected to end level, and Czech Republic's combined chances (draw plus win) approach 30%, indicating the match retains genuine competitive uncertainty despite the favorite's edge.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge markedly. The market places Mexico at 51% and Czech Republic at 22%, a gap of roughly 19 percentage points from the model's respective estimates. Polymarket prices align closely with the broader sportsbook view. This dashboard presents both forecasts; it does not adjudicate which better reflects the true match probability.
